---
title: "ML Course — Presentation"
author: "Your Name"
date: today
format:
  beamer:
    theme: Madrid
    colortheme: beaver
    fonttheme: structurebold
    aspectratio: 169
    keep-tex: false
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
jupyter: python3
bibliography: references.bib
---

## Agenda

- Motivation
- Data
- Method
- Results

## Motivation

- State the research question [@hastie2009]
- @hastie2009 frame this as a bias-variance tradeoff

## Data

```{python}
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

rng = np.random.default_rng(42)
df = pd.DataFrame({"x": rng.uniform(0, 10, 50),
                   "y": rng.normal(5, 2, 50)})
df.head(4)
```

## Visualization

```{python}
#| echo: false
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(5, 3))
ax.scatter(df["x"], df["y"], alpha=0.7)
ax.set(xlabel="x", ylabel="y")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Method

\begin{columns}
\begin{column}{0.5\textwidth}
  \textbf{Approach}
  \begin{itemize}
    \item Linear regression
    \item OLS estimation
  \end{itemize}
\end{column}
\begin{column}{0.5\textwidth}
  \textbf{Assumptions}
  \begin{itemize}
    \item Linearity
    \item Homoskedasticity
  \end{itemize}
\end{column}
\end{columns}

## Results

```{python}
from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression

m = LinearRegression().fit(df[["x"]], df["y"])
print(f"Intercept: {m.intercept_:.2f},  Slope: {m.coef_[0]:.2f}")
```

## Conclusion

- Key takeaway 1
- Key takeaway 2

## References
